## Structural Insights

- 9 file(s) touched across source, test (+146 / -83).
- New public API surface: handleSidebarToggle.
- Deterministic type: feat (model will confirm).

## Commit Message

```
feat(admin): Introduce navigation sidebar and related CSS/JS

This change introduces a new navigation sidebar within the Django admin interface. The sidebar is implemented with updated JavaScript (`nav_sidebar.js`) and CSS (`sidebar.css`), enhancing user experience by providing easier access to different sections of the admin panel.  The changes also include modifications to `options.py` to include the necessary CSS and Javascript files, as well as updates to templates for base layout and change lists. Finally, adjustments were made to responsive CSS to prevent horizontal scrolling issues when using the sidebar.
```

## Changelog

### Unreleased

#### Added or Changed
- Added a `sidebar.css` stylesheet (previously `nav_sidebar.css`) in the admin static files, incorporating styles for the navigation sidebar.
- Added a JavaScript file (`nav_sidebar.js`) to handle the toggle functionality of the navigation sidebar.
- Modified `options.py` to include CSS and Javascript references for the new sidebar.
- Updated `base.html` template to conditionally load the `nav_sidebar.css` and `nav_sidebar.js` files when the navigation sidebar is enabled.
- Updated `change_list.html` template to adjust layout for the presence of a sidebar.
- Updated `nav_sidebar.html` template to change the class name on the toggle button from "toggle-nav-sidebar" to "toggle-sidebar".
- Modified `changelists.css` to prevent horizontal scrollbar issues when using sidebars.
- Modified `responsive.css` to adjust layout for smaller screens with sidebars.
- Updated tests in `tests/admin_views/tests.py` to account for changes related to the navigation sidebar.
